registry.description: Contentment is the inner state of being satisfied with what one has — not because there is nothing more to desire but because one has come to a settled acceptance of one's present condition. The Greek term (autarkeia) means self-sufficiency in the Stoic sense, but Paul redefines it: not independence from need but the learned ability to be at rest whether in abundance or in want. Contentment is not apathy but a trained inner freedom from the tyranny of circumstances.

terms.lsj_entry:
αὐτάρκ-εια, ἡ, 
self-sufficiency, independence, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__II concrete, a sufficiency, [Refs 3rd c.AD+]; a competence, [Refs 2nd c.AD+]
terms.short_def_mounce: contentment, having all of one’s needs, sufficiency

senses.sense_text:
contentment, having all of one’s needs, sufficiency 
a competence of the necessaries of life, sufficiency 2Cor. 9:8; a frame of mind viewing one's lot as sufficient, contentedness, 1Tim. 6:6*

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 9:8 And God is able to make all grace abound to you , so that having all sufficiency in all things at all times , you may abound in every good work .
1Ti 6:6 But godliness with contentment is great gain ,

terms.lsj_entry:
αὐτάρκ-ης, ες, 
(ἀρκέω) sufficient in oneself, self-supporting, independent of others, ἀνθρώπου σῶμα ἓν οὐδὲν αὔ. ἐστιν[Refs 5th c.BC+]; εἰς εὐδαιμονίαν, of ἀρετή, [Refs 5th c.BC+]helping itself, acting instinctively, [Refs 4th c.BC+]; αὐ. βοή a self-reliant shout, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; οἰκία -έστερον ἑνός, πόλις δ᾽ οἰκίας[Refs 4th c.BC+]; σῶμα αὔ. πρός τι strong enough for a thing, [Refs 5th c.BC+]: with infinitive, able of oneself to do a thing, εἰ γὰρ αὐτάρκη τὰ ψηφίσματα ἦν ἢ ὑμᾶς ἀναγκάζειν κτλ.[Refs 5th c.BC+]. adverb -κως, ἔχειν[Refs 4th c.BC+]: superlative -έστατα, ζῆν[Refs 5th c.BC+]
__II sufficient in quantity, ἀργύριον αὔ. εἰς σιτωνίαν[Refs 1st c.AD+]; ὄξους τὸ -έστατον[Refs 2nd c.AD+]adverb -κως sufficiently, [Refs 1st c.AD+]

senses.sense_text:
content; (possibly) self-sufficient 
primarily sufficient or adequate in one's self; contented with one's lot, Phil. 4:11*

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 4:40 where they found rich , good pasture , and the land was very broad , quiet , and peaceful , for the former inhabitants there belonged to Ham .
Job 16:12 I was at ease , and he broke me apart; he seized me by the neck and dashed me to pieces ; he set me up as his target ;
Job 20:20 “ Because he knew no contentment in his belly , he will not let anything in which he delights escape him.
Job 21:23 One dies in his full vigor , being wholly at ease and secure ,
Psa 73:12 Behold , these are the wicked ; always at ease , they increase in riches .
Jer 49:31 “Rise up , advance against a nation at ease, that dwells securely , declares the Lord , that has no gates or bars , that dwells alone .

Eze 23:42 The sound of a carefree multitude was with her; and with men of the common sort , drunkards were brought from the wilderness ; and they put bracelets on the hands of the women, and beautiful crowns on their heads .

Zec 7:7 Were not these the words that the Lord proclaimed by the former prophets , when Jerusalem was inhabited and prosperous , with her cities around her, and the South and the lowland were inhabited ?'”

verses.verse_text: Psa 122:7 Peace be within your walls and security within your towers !”
Pro 1:32 For the simple are killed by their turning away , and the complacency of fools destroys them ;
Pro 17:1 Better is a dry morsel with quiet than a house full of feasting with strife .
Jer 22:21 I spoke to you in your prosperity , but you said , ‘I will not listen .’ This has been your way from your youth , that you have not obeyed my voice .
Eze 16:49 Behold , this was the guilt of your sister Sodom : she and her daughters had pride , excess of food , and prosperous ease , but did not aid the poor and needy .

Dan 8:25 By his cunning he shall make deceit prosper under his hand , and in his own mind he shall become great . Without warning he shall destroy many . And he shall even rise up against the Prince of princes , and he shall be broken —but by no human hand .
Dan 11:21 In his place shall arise a contemptible person to whom royal majesty has not been given . He shall come in without warning and obtain the kingdom by flatteries .

Dan 11:24 Without warning he shall come into the richest parts of the province , and he shall do what neither his fathers nor his fathers ’ fathers have done , scattering among them plunder , spoil , and goods . He shall devise plans against strongholds , but only for a time .
